additional_metadata.note: Held as part of Toronto Tech Week 2026.

events.agenda: 6:00 Arrive, Eat, Drink, Socialize; 6:30 Speaker 1 and 2 presentations.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data. Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, AI, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
events.event_name: dbt Meetup, Toronto Tech Week 2026 Edition!
events.location: OneEleven, 325 Front Street W, Suite 4, Toronto, ON
events.raw_text:
Fri, May 29, 2026 · 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM EDT

1st Speaker: Josh Harris, founder of AnswerLayer. Topic: Open Semantic Interchange (OSI v1.0), what it is, why it exists, and what it means for dbt users.
2nd Speaker: Daria Sukhareva, creator of kWwhat. Topic: grounding ChatBI with dimensional models, semantic models, and rules.

talks.description: An overview of the Open Semantic Interchange standard shipped in January 2026 with dbt Labs as a co-leading vendor, what it is, why it exists, and what it means for dbt users.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Topic: Open Semantic Interchange shipped in January 2026 with dbt Labs as one of the co-leading vendors. OSI v1.0: what it is, why it exists, and what it means for dbt users.
talks.title: Open Semantic Interchange (OSI v1.0) and What It Means for dbt Users

talks.description: Grounding ChatBI (conversational AI-driven BI) with dimensional models, semantic models, and rules, arguing AI shouldn't be connecting dots on its own.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Topic: Let's chat: grounding ChatBI with dimensional models, semantic models, and rules - why your AI shouldn't be connecting dots.
talks.title: Grounding ChatBI with Dimensional Models, Semantic Models, and Rules

events.agenda: 6:00-6:30 Check in/Registration; 6:30-6:35 Welcome Remarks; 6:35-7:00 dbt's Collective Magic talk + Q&A; 7:00-7:25 Metricflow + dbt-core OSS talk + Q&A; 7:25-7:30 Closing Remarks; 7:30-8:00 Networking.
events.event_description: dbt Meetups are networking events open to all folks working with data! Talks predominantly focus on community members' experience with dbt, however, you'll catch presentations on broader topics such as analytics engineering, data stacks, data ops, modeling, testing, and team structures.
events.location: New Stadium, 83 Walnut Avenue, Toronto, ON
events.raw_text:
Tue, Nov 25, 2025 · 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM EST

6:35 - 7:00 | dbt's Collective Magic - Eddy +Q&A
7:00 - 7:25 | Metricflow + dbt-core OSS: past, present, future + Q&A - Michelle

talks.description: A talk on the collective magic of the dbt community and ecosystem.
talks.title: dbt's Collective Magic

talks.description: The past, present, and future of MetricFlow and dbt-core as open source software.
talks.raw_text_excerpt: Metricflow + dbt-core OSS: past, present, future + Q&A -Michelle (25 min)
talks.title: Metricflow + dbt-core OSS: Past, Present, Future

additional_metadata.venue_host: Village by the Grange Food Court, Toronto

events.agenda: 6:00-6:30pm Check-in/Order Food/Icebreakers; 6:30-6:45pm Welcome Remarks & calls for speakers/hosts; 6:45-7:45pm Dinner/Dessert/Networking; 7:45-8:00pm Wrap up.
events.event_description: A casual evening of food, fun, and dbt with no slides and no talks—just good food and great conversations with other analytics engineers and data folks in Toronto's growing dbt network.
events.event_name: Analytics After Hours: Eat, Drink, Socialize
events.location: Village by the Grange Food Court, 105 McCaul Street, Toronto, ON
events.raw_text:
Wed, Sep 17, 2025 · 6:00 PM to 8:00 PM EDT

No slides, no talks—just good food and great conversations with other analytics engineers and data folks.
